sls.tf
Home Documentation GitHub

Installation

How to install and set up sls.tf in your project

Prerequisites

Before you begin, make sure you have the following tools installed:

  • Terraform (v1.0+) - For infrastructure deployment
  • Node.js (v16+) - For development and testing
  • Git - For version control
  • AWS CLI - Configured with appropriate credentials

Quick Start

The fastest way to get started with sls.tf is to add it as a submodule to your existing project:

Add sls.tf submodule

# Add the sls.tf submodule to your project
git submodule add https://github.com/your-org/sls.tf.git modules/sls.tf
git submodule update --init --recursive

Initialize Terraform

# Navigate to your project directory
cd your-project

# Initialize Terraform
terraform init

# Plan your infrastructure
terraform plan

# Apply the changes
terraform apply

Manual Installation

If you prefer not to use Git submodules, you can manually download and include sls.tf:

Download and setup

# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/your-org/sls.tf.git

# Copy to your project modules directory
cp -r sls.tf /path/to/your-project/modules/

# Navigate to your project
cd /path/to/your-project

# Initialize Terraform
terraform init

Basic Configuration

Create a simple Terraform configuration to use sls.tf:

main.tf

terraform {'{'}
  required_version = ">= 1.0"
  required_providers {'{'}
    aws = {'{'}
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "~> 5.0"
    {'}'}
  {'}'}
{'}'}

provider "aws" {'{'}
  region = "us-east-1"
{'}'}

module "serverless" {'{'}
  source = "./modules/sls.tf"

  # Path to your serverless.yml file
  config_file = "${path.root}/serverless.yml"

  # AWS region for deployment
  region = "us-east-1"

  # Environment variables
  environment = "production"
{'}'}

Configuration File

Make sure you have a serverless.yml file in your project root:

serverless.yml

service: my-api
provider:
  name: aws
  runtime: nodejs18.x
  region: us-east-1

functions:
  hello:
    handler: handler.hello
    events:
      - http:
          path: hello
          method: get

Verification

After installation, verify everything is working correctly:

Test the installation

# Check Terraform configuration
terraform validate

# See what will be created
terraform plan

# If everything looks good, apply
terraform apply

Next Steps

Once you have sls.tf installed and working, check out these guides:

  • Configuration Guide - Advanced configuration options
  • Basic Example - Complete working example
  • TypeScript Support - Using TypeScript configurations
